intro: Established in 1977, the Department of Records and Information Services (DORIS) preserves and provides public access to the historical and contemporary records of New York City government. It operates the Municipal Archives, the Municipal Library (founded in 1913), and the Records Center, maintaining collections that range from birth, death, and marriage records to historical maps, ledgers, and images dating back to 1645.

cardHighlights.function: Preserves and provides access to NYC government records
cardHighlights.scale: Preserves more than 200,000 cubic feet of historical documents
